explanation: The property requires that, once the PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) mode is selected, it should always be the case that the inspiratory pressure is set to the targeted value for PCV mode (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV). This captures the natural language requirement that the inspiratory pressure in PCV mode is controlled by the P_inspPCV parameter and kept constant, as the atomic proposition indicates that the inspiratory valve pressure is set to the correct PCV target. This persistency ("kept constant") is best represented with an invariant (globally) implication: whenever PCVModeSelected is true, P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV should also be true at every moment from then on.

The requirement specifies that in PCV Mode, the inspiratory pressure must be regulated by the Inspiratory Pressure parameter (P_inspPCV), and that pressure should remain constant. The atomic proposition P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV directly represents the system setting the valve to the PCV-mode pressure. Therefore, the LTL property should state that as long as the system is in PCV Mode (PCVMode), it must always ensure the inspiratory valve pressure is set to the target pressure (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV) throughout operation. This is represented by the invariant: when PCVMode is active, always (globally) P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV is true. This is best captured by template 17 (G (bool_exp2 -> (G bool_exp3))), where bool_exp2 is 'PCVMode' and bool_exp3 is 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV'. N_DURATION is not needed for this property.

The requirement states that in PCV Mode, the inspiratory pressure must be controlled and kept constant at the level set by the Inspiratory Pressure parameter (P_inspPCV). The atomic proposition 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV' directly indicates whether the inspiratory pressure is set to the targeted PCV value. To express that this must always be maintained, the appropriate LTL pattern is to globally (G) require that this condition holds at all times. This captures both the requirement of control and of being constant, as any deviation from the target value at any time would violate the property.

The requirement states that in PCV Mode, the target inspiratory pressure must be set by the 'P_inspPCV' parameter and it is kept constant. In the provided atomic propositions, 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV' means the inspiratory valve pressure is set to the target inspiratory pressure of PCV mode, capturing this control and level. To capture the requirement that this holds whenever PCV mode is active, I use a global (always) response: whenever PCVMode is active, it is always the case that P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV holds, i.e., the inspiratory pressure is set and maintained at the PCV target value. This captures the idea of being 'kept constant' by ensuring the property always holds in 'PCVMode', not just at transition points.

The natural language requirement states that in PCV Mode, the inspiratory pressure level must be controlled by the "Inspiratory Pressure" parameter (P_inspPCV), and should be kept constant. The atomic proposition 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV' indicates that the inspiratory valve pressure is set to the target inspiratory pressure of PCV mode (i.e., it matches the PCV parameter). To capture the requirement that this should always be the case (kept constant), we select an LTL formula that globally (always) maintains this condition. Thus, we use template 7, which asserts that 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV' is always true during operation.

The requirement states that in PCV Mode, the inspiratory valve pressure (P_insp) must be set and controlled by the parameter P_inspPCV and is kept constant. This means that, whenever the PCV Mode is active, the inspiratory valve pressure should always be set to the value of P_inspPCV. The atomic proposition 'PCVMode' signals when PCV mode is active, and 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV' signals when P_insp is at the target value for PCV Mode. Therefore, the LTL property should globally ensure that whenever PCVMode is active, P_insp is always set to P_inspPCV (i.e., remains true as long as PCVMode is active). Template 37, G (bool_exp1 -> (G bool_exp3)), is chosen to express that whenever PCVMode is true, always (globally) P_insp is set to P_inspPCV.

The requirement is that in PCV mode (Pressure Controlled Ventilation), the inspiratory pressure should be controlled by the Inspiratory Pressure parameter (P_inspPCV) and kept constant. The atomic proposition 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV' directly denotes that the inspiratory valve pressure is set to this target level. To capture the idea that this is maintained consistently (kept constant), we use the globally operator (G) to require that the condition (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV) should always hold whenever the system is in PCV mode (PCVMode). Thus, the property expresses: 'Always, if the system is in PCV mode, then the inspiratory valve pressure is set to the target PCV inspiratory pressure.'

The requirement states that in PCV Mode, the inspiratory pressure should be set according to the 'Inspiratory Pressure parameter (P_inspPCV)' and kept constant. This translates to ensuring that whenever the system is in PCV mode, the actual inspiratory valve pressure is always set to the target pressure for PCV, i.e., P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV should always be true whenever PCVMode is active. This is best captured by 'Globally, if PCVMode is active, then P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV should hold', which matches template 30. This ensures the controller is always setting the inspiratory valve pressure as required when in PCV Mode.

The requirement states that in PCV Mode, the inspiratory pressure should always be kept at the target value set by the P_inspPCV parameter. The atomic proposition 'PCVMode' indicates the PCV mode is active, and 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV' indicates the pressure is set to P_inspPCV. Thus, globally (i.e. always), whenever PCVMode is active, the pressure should be equal to P_inspPCV. This is captured by the LTL formula: G (PCVMode -> P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV) (template 30), which ensures that if 'PCVMode' holds at any time, then 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV' must also hold at that time.

The property describes that, globally (i.e., at all times), if the PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) Mode is active, then the inspiratory pressure is set to the target inspiratory pressure for PCV mode (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV). This directly implements the natural language requirement that 'the target inspiratory pressure level shall be controlled by the Inspiratory Pressure parameter (P_inspPCV) and is kept constant' while in PCV mode.
